response.reasoning: Step 1: Experience length — the candidate has roughly 17-20 years of professional software development (2006-present), meeting the 15-year minimum. Step 2: Seniority — the role is a Director-level IC (Principal Engineer). The candidate's highest titles are Senior/Staff Software Engineer; there is no evidence of principal-level scope, org-wide technical leadership, or strategy ownership. Step 3: AI depth — the role wants recognized AI expertise with demonstrated AI products/platforms. The candidate has recent, adjacent exposure (MCP code generators, AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq since 2025) but no shipped AI products, ML background, publications, or recognized AI standing. Step 4: Cloud — practical cloud skills exist (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, microservices at scale at Dunnhumby), solid but hands-on rather than strategic/advisory. Step 5: Entrepreneurial signal — founder of GISCollective (open-source geo platform, 2016-present) is a genuine plus, though it is a side project rather than intrapreneurship at scale. Step 6: Education — BS Computer Science (MIT) satisfies the minimum; no Master's/PhD in an AI field. Note the MIT dates (2009-2012) overlap fully with concurrent jobs in Bucharest/Toronto, which warrants verification. Step 7: Influence/inspiration — conference talks (FOSS4G) and workshops show teaching and community engagement, but at a practitioner level, not industry-thought-leader level. Overall: a strong, versatile senior engineer, but two levels below the role's seniority and missing the core AI-strategy credentials.
strengths: 17+ years of broad engineering experience across web, embedded, low-latency, and cloud systems, exceeding the 15-year minimum
Founder experience (GISCollective) plus recent hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling work
Demonstrated knowledge-sharing through conference talks, workshops, and courses, relevant to the evangelism aspect of the role
concerns: No principal/director-level track record — career has plateaued at senior/staff IC without evidence of org-wide strategic influence
AI expertise is recent and tooling-adjacent, not the recognized, product-proven AI leadership the role requires
MIT degree dates (2009-2012) fully overlap with simultaneous full-time jobs in other countries, requiring verification

key_factors.factor: Seniority gap vs. Director-level IC role
Meets experience minimum with founder/startup background

response.justification: The candidate comfortably clears the experience and education minimums and brings genuine breadth, founder experience, and recent AI-agent tooling exposure. However, this is a Director-level AI strategy role demanding recognized AI expertise and demonstrated AI products at scale, and the résumé shows a strong senior/staff-level generalist rather than a principal-level AI leader. The seniority and AI-credibility gaps are decisive, though the profile is close enough on fundamentals that a screen could be defensible if the pipeline is thin.
